methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Sicona Battery Technologies is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Christiaan Jordaan and Andrew Minett, having raised US$22M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe comprising one public comparable (ticker: LNZA) and eight private companies operating in the same sector, including Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy, TAE Technologies, and Redwood Materials.

The peer-median method was applied using three same-sector, same-stage comparables. Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy carry Series A valuations of $200M, $150M, and $110M respectively, establishing a range of $110M to $200M with a median of $150M. This represents the primary triangulation input given the limited availability of alternative valuation methodologies applicable at this stage and with this dataset.

The analysis supports a median valuation of $150M with a calibrated range of $128M (low) to $172M (high). Key uncertainties include the limited single-method triangulation, the nascent nature of battery technology ventures (which may inflate peer valuations relative to near-term cash generation), and potential divergence between Sicona's technical differentiation and the broader peer set. The valuation should be revisited as additional comparable transactions emerge and the company approaches Series B.
